svg.text: Insert into a sorted list of intervals
Input is already sorted and disjoint; insert one new interval, return merged result.
1. Append all intervals ending strictly before new.start (they cannot overlap)
2. While next interval overlaps new, absorb its bounds (extend new.start/end)
3. Append the absorbed new, then append remaining non-overlapping intervals
Time: O(n) — no sort needed because input is pre-sorted.
Sweep-line: convert each interval into two events (+1 at start, −1 at end).
Sort events; sweep, tracking running active count. Max active = max overlap.
Heap-of-ends: for "meeting rooms II" — sort intervals by start, push end
into min-heap, pop when next start ≥ heap top (room freed). Heap size = rooms.
Both run in O(n log n). Sweep-line is more flexible (handles many event types);
heap-of-ends is more direct for room/resource allocation.
Choose the variant that matches the question — merge, count overlap, or schedule.
